summary: Alfred is a terminal UI editor with minimal observability instrumentation. There is no structured logging framework, no distributed tracing, no metrics collection, and no health checks. Error handling is basic with Result-based error propagation. As a single-process synchronous TUI application, observability demands are different from distributed systems, but internal state transitions lack visibility.

logging.notes: Only eprintln! used for error reporting to stderr on startup; no runtime logging; no structured logging framework (log, tracing, slog); Plugin load errors surface via UI messages, not logs; File I/O errors use thiserror enums but aren't logged

tracing.notes: No distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry, Jaeger, Zipkin); single-process synchronous application; no request/span instrumentation needed; key domain transitions (buffer mutations, mode changes) lack visibility hooks

metrics.notes: No metrics collection (Prometheus, StatsD, Micrometer); no counters, gauges, or histograms; no performance telemetry for buffer operations, rendering, or Lisp evaluation

health_checks.notes: Desktop application; not applicable for TUI editor; no health check endpoints; terminal availability implicit

recommendations.title: Add structured logging for plugin load failures
recommendations.description: Plugin errors currently surface only as UI messages. Add structured logging (log or tracing crate) to capture plugin discovery, load order resolution, and init errors with context. This aids debugging plugin interactions and dependency issues.

recommendations.title: Instrument hook and command execution for visibility
recommendations.description: Key domain transitions (hooks firing, commands executing) lack visibility. Consider adding optional debug logging (gated by feature flag) to trace hook lifecycle and command dispatch paths.

recommendations.title: Log buffer state changes in debug builds
recommendations.description: Large buffer mutations (insert, delete, undo/redo) are silent. Add optional debug instrumentation to log transaction boundaries and state snapshots.

recommendations.title: Document error propagation patterns
recommendations.description: Error handling uses thiserror enums and Result types consistently, but no observability of where errors originate. Document error paths and consider adding span context.

recommendations.title: Consider metrics for performance-critical paths
recommendations.description: Once feature gates are added, collect latency metrics for syntax highlighting, buffer operations, and Lisp evaluation to identify hotspots.
